questions.q: 25 g of a mixture containing $\mathrm{NaCl}, \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$ and $\mathrm{CaCO}_3$ is heated with dil HCl produces $8.8 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{CO}_2$ gas. 50 g of the same mixture on dry heating gives $8.8 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{CO}_2$ gas. Percentage composition of $\mathrm{NaCl}, \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$ and $\mathrm{CaCO}_3$ respectively is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Mass of } \mathrm{NaCl}^{-} \mathrm{ag} \\ & \text { Mass of } \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3=\mathrm{b} \mathrm{g} \\ & \text { Mass of } \mathrm{CaCO}_3=\mathrm{c} \mathrm{g} \\ & \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b}+\mathrm{c}=25 \mathrm{~g} \\ & \frac{\mathrm{~b}}{106}+\frac{\mathrm{c}}{100}=\frac{8.8}{44}=0.2 \\ & \frac{2 \mathrm{c}}{100}=0.2 \\ & \frac{\mathrm{c}}{100}=0.1 \\ & \mathrm{c}=10 \mathrm{gm} \\ & \mathrm{b}=10.6 \mathrm{gm} \\ & \mathrm{a}=4.4 \mathrm{gm}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The composition of a sample of Wustite is $\mathrm{Fe}_{0.93} \mathrm{O}$. What is the percentage of iron present as $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}$ ?
questions.solution:
The total charge of $\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$ and $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}$ should be 2 .
Let, the percentage of $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}=\mathrm{x}$
Hence, $3 \times x+2(0.93-x)=2$
$\therefore x=0.14$
$\%$ of $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}=\frac{0.14}{0.93} \times 100=15 \%$
Hence, (A) is the correct answer.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
Consider the reaction given below
$\mathrm{I}_2+5 \mathrm{Cl}_2+6 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{HIO}_3+10 \mathrm{HCl}$
When 3 mole $\mathrm{I}_2, 2$ mole $\mathrm{Cl}_2$ and 3 mole $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ are reacted to give $80 \%$ yield. Incorrect statement among given options is
options: $\mathrm{I}_2$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ are left out after the reaction
questions.solution:
Limiting reagent is $\mathrm{Cl}_2 .0 .8$ mole $\mathrm{HIO}_3$ would be formed if $100 \%$ yield and 0.64 mole with $80 \%$ yield.
3.2 mole of HCl is formed with $80 \%$ yield.
